title: document model structure has metadata specifications and modules
content:
# document model structure has metadata specifications and modules

A document model consists of three layers:

1. **Basic Metadata** — `id`, `name`, `extension`, `description`, `author` (name + website)
2. **Specifications** — Versioned specs containing `version`, `changeLog`, and `state` definitions (global/local with schema, initialValue, examples)
3. **Modules** — Operational modules that group related operations

The metadata identifies the model. The specifications define its state shape. The modules organize what operations can be performed on that state.

Since [[document models have 37 available operations across 7 categories]], the operations for building a document model span header setup, module creation, operation definition, and state schema configuration.

---

Topics:
- [[schema design]]
description: Core components: basic metadata (id, name, extension), versioned specifications (state with global/local schemas), and operational modules
